_instructions.purpose: Session bootstrap. Fetch at session start. Meta operational chunks (behaviour_personality, workflow) load from GitHub. Project knowledge queries go to Supabase REST API — see supabase block.
_instructions.posture: Be bold with loading. When evidence points clearly to a node or query, execute it. Prefer action over confirmation.

operation_model.online: Fast operations: Supabase REST queries for project knowledge, GitHub fetch for meta chunks. Do these freely.
operation_model.offline: Heavy operations: Supabase UPDATE/INSERT (via SQL editor), GitHub writes (loader/meta chunks). Deliberate and explicit only.

_instructions.session_start_acknowledgement: After loading, always include this line in the acknowledgement: 'Type: load: meta.workflow to load working style and session rules.'
how_to_load: 0. At session start: check for session intent declaration. Recognized intents: 'kb review', 'knowledge base review', 'memory review', 'database review'. If declared, acknowledge and suspend session ceiling.

1. At session start: fetch session_loader.json via GitHub MCP or web_fetch raw_base. Auto-load behaviour_personality from GitHub (see github block). Report load count in acknowledgement. Append session_start_acknowledgement line.
1b. Cascading sub-chunk load: if a GitHub meta chunk contains '_sub_chunks', load them immediately. Counts toward auto-load ceiling.

2. For ANY project knowledge request (perspective, blob_world, ecosystem_ccg, etc.): query Supabase REST API (see supabase block). Use web_fetch with apikey and Authorization headers. Do NOT fetch the old GitHub chunk files for project content — they are the legacy system.

3. Common Supabase query patterns are in supabase.query_patterns. Replace {project} and {kind} with actual values. Prefer specific filters over broad selects. Surface only nodes relevant to the question.
4. For meta operational content (behaviour_personality, workflow, session_lock): fetch from GitHub chunk files (see github block). These are config, not project knowledge.
5. After querying: summarise kind + count of results, then show relevant titles and key body content. Do not dump raw JSON unless asked.
6. Context ceiling: Supabase queries are on-demand — do not pre-emptively load everything. Query specifically. Only fetch edges if the user asks about relationships.
7. NEVER fetch anything from _archive/. Never use old GitHub chunk files for project knowledge lookups.

lookup_syntax.query: query: <project> <kind> — translates to Supabase REST query
lookup_syntax.update: update: <project>.<kind>.<id> — user runs SQL in Supabase editor
lookup_syntax.new: new node: <project>.<kind> — user inserts via Supabase editor or API

logging_format.tier_1_decisions: Architectural and design decisions. Fields: decision, rationale (one sentence), affects (node kind + project). Written at end of session.
logging_format.tier_2_implementation: Implementation-phase entries. Fields: attempted, outcome, decided. Tag with project.

github.use_for: Meta operational chunks only: behaviour_personality, workflow, session_lock, governance. Not for project knowledge.
github.fallback: If GitHub MCP unavailable, construct URLs as raw_base + chunk_path and use web_fetch.

schema.nodes: id, project, kind (decision|invariant|open_question|finding|definition|framework|rule_state|task|pitfall), title, body (jsonb), status (active|superseded|open|resolved|deferred|rejected|obsolete|in_progress|done), tags (text[]), legacy_chunk, legacy_id, source_session

supabase.note: Fill anon_key from: Supabase Dashboard → Settings → API → Project API keys → anon public. Replace {project} and {kind} with actual values in queries above.

meta._summary: Memory system metadata: versioning, global tags, system info, behaviour rules, workflow, session lock data.

auto_load_governance.auto_load_ceiling_note: Maximum auto-load chunks beyond session_loader. Currently using 2 (behaviour_personality + governance via cascading). Headroom for 2 more before warning triggers. Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides, decision logged in versioning.
auto_load_governance.session_chunk_ceiling_note: Maximum total chunks loaded in a single session. Soft ceiling — Claude warns, user decides.
auto_load_governance.kb_review_exemption: Declaring session intent as KB review suspends the session chunk ceiling for that session.
auto_load_governance.kb_definition: KB = Knowledge Base. Project knowledge is in Supabase. Meta config is in GitHub chunks.

notes._summary: General working notes, memory system drafts, session logs (cross-project).

entities._summary: Named entities not tied to a specific project: people, organizations, artifacts.

projects._summary: All project-specific content. Knowledge lives in Supabase nodes — query via REST API.

perspective._summary: Representation Space Traversal AI — a novel architecture where traversal of data representation space is the primary computational mechanism.
perspective._note: Query authoritative nodes first: ?project=eq.perspective&tags=cs.{authoritative}. These win over any conflicting nodes on the same topic.

blob_world._summary: Analytically deformable star-shaped soft-body creature system. Transported local coordinate frames create curved pseudo-appendages. Compact parameterization, analytical collision, GPU rendering. Target: ALife/evolutionary simulation.
blob_world._note: Resume point: rendering fiber artifact deferred, physics next.

ecosystem_ccg._summary: Ecosystem-building CCG. Players build shared biomes and food webs against an escalating world deck. Organisms have fitness-based population dynamics, resource needs, and trait systems.
ecosystem_ccg._note: game.rs/ai.rs/ui.rs not yet updated to v4 types — first task next session.

children_of_battle._summary: Mobile tactical roguelike with generational breeding, deck-building combat, and permadeath.

hextorussim._note: No chunks yet — add subtopics and summary when KB writes begin.
